content: Just as the linguistic definition of reflection frames it as both a light/energy reflection (passive signal redirection) and active deliberate introspection, LLM self-reflection mechanisms can be split into two understudied modes that combine these two properties: passive 'reflexion' as mirroring inconsistent solving perspectives against an agent’s raw generated outputs, and active reflection as deliberate self-critique. This hybrid reflexion-reflection framework can resolve the common failure mode of LLM self-reflection where agents reinforce incorrect prior reasoning instead of correcting it.
